data.title: Oracle Stock (ORCL) Takes a Hit after Delaying the Completion of OpenAI Data Centers
data.description:
Tech giant Oracle (ORCL) is delaying the completion of several data centers it's building for AI firm OpenAI (PC:OPAIQ), thereby pushing the timeline from 2027 to 2028, according to Bloomberg News. The delays are mainly due to shortages in labor and materials, based on comments from people familiar with the matter.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

These centers are part of a $300 billion deal that Oracle signed earlier this year to supply computing power for OpenAI's artificial intelligence models. Nevertheless, even with the setbacks, the U.S.-based data centers are still on a fast-track schedule, especially when considering that they are expected to be among the largest in the world. However, investor worries about slow returns from massive AI investments have already hurt Oracle's stock.

After a 13% drop earlier in the week, the stock fell again today following the news of the delays. Still, not all progress is stalled. Oracle Co-CEO Clay Magouyrk confirmed during an earnings call that construction on the first data center in Abilene, Texas, is going well, with over 96,000 Nvidia (NVDA) chips already delivered for use in OpenAI's projects.

Is Oracle a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on ORCL stock based on 22 Buys, 11 Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average ORCL price target of $302.10 per share implies 59.7% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: U.S. Equity Funds Break Three Week Streak of Outflows
data.description:
U.S. equity funds broke a three-week streak of outflows ahead of the Fed's decision to cut interest rates by 25 bps.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

For the week ended December 10, investors purchased a net of $3.3 billion in U.S. equity funds after selling $3.52 billion during the prior week, according to data from LSEG Lipper. U.S. funds that track a particular sector saw strong demand with $2.81 billion in net inflows, the highest since the week ended October 22.

Global, U.S. Equity Funds Attract Billions in Weekly Inflows

U.S. metals and mining funds saw inflows of $672 million, while industrials added $548 million. On the other hand, money market funds saw a net of $4.58 billion in outflows as investors pressed the sell button ahead of the expected Fed rate cut.

It wasn't only U.S. equity funds that were in high demand. Global equity funds added a net of $12.9 billion in weekly inflows, led by European funds at $6.4 billion after a $6.47 billion rise during the previous week. Furthermore, Emerging markets funds rose by $2.78 billion, while Asian funds added $1.3 billion.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Ripple Just Landed Its First European Bank. Will This Pull XRP's Price Out of Its $2 Grave?
data.description:
Ripple has secured its first European bank client, AMINA Bank, to deploy its licensed Ripple Payments platform. This move is a big step as it shows that regulated European banks are now moving crypto payments from experimental labs into their core infrastructure, which could have a profound effect on the utility and price of XRP (XRP-USD).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Why Demand for XRP Could Surge

The biggest factor influencing XRP's price is its utility as a bridge currency for instant cross-border transfers. While the current news focuses on AMINA using fiat and stablecoins (like Ripple's own RLUSD) for flows, this adoption validates the core Ripple payments technology. As more banks like AMINA integrate Ripple Payments, they are validating a system that bypasses slow, expensive correspondent banking networks.

Ripple's system allows value to move directly and settle within minutes, unlike traditional networks that use multiple intermediaries. This model naturally favors using a highly liquid, fast, and low-fee digital asset, which is a role XRP is designed for. Widespread adoption of the payment rail itself puts massive, sustained pressure on liquidity providers to hold and utilize XRP to service these instant transfers, increasing demand and potentially driving up the price.

Regulatory Clarity Pushes Banks to "Production-Grade" Use

The partnership highlights a key trend that is bullish for all regulated digital assets. AMINA is a FINMA-regulated digital asset bank in Switzerland, which means this adoption is compliant and integrated directly with traditional banking rails. The move tells us that banks are integrating blockchain settlement without leaving regulatory frameworks. This regulatory clarity, especially in Europe, is pushing banks from "experimentation to production-grade blockchain use cases".

This environment is important for XRP because it moves the asset out of the "crypto speculation" category and into the "institutional treasury and payments infrastructure" category.

The more regulated banks (like AMINA, which already supports Ripple's stablecoin RLUSD) integrate the network, the stronger Ripple's position in Europe becomes, strengthening the fundamental case for XRP's long-term utility.

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $1.9962.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: S&P 500 to Power to 7,600 in 2026, Says Goldman Sachs
data.description:
2026 will be another positive year for the stock market, according to Goldman Sachs. On Friday, the firm set its 2026 S&P 500 (SPX) price target at 7,600, implying upside of 10% from Thursday's close.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Goldman Chief U.S. equity strategist Ben Snider expects AI productivity gains to provide a tailwind to earnings and forecasts the benchmark index to grow its EPS by 12% to $305. Snider expects Big Tech to drive a majority of the earnings growth, although he also expects broader participation from the rest of the index.

Goldman Warns on Profit Margins and Slower Easing Cycle

However, Goldman notes that risks to the S&P 500 include a slow interest rate easing cycle and lower profit margins. On Wednesday, the Fed voted 9-3 to cut rates by 25 bps, although two officials dissented in favor of an unchanged rate, while Fed Governor Stephen Miran called for a 50 bps reduction. The number of dissents was the largest since 2019, reflecting a split central bank and casting doubt on accelerated rate cuts.

Besides tech, Goldman also expects strong earnings growth from the industrials, materials, and utilities sectors. In recent days, the market has been experiencing a shift from growth to value, with the Dow Jones (DJIA) up by 1.4% during the past week and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) down by 1.6%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Costco Stock (COST) Drops After Company Fails to Announce a Special Dividend
data.description:
Costco's (COST) stock is down about 2% a day after the retailer delivered strong quarterly financial results and bullish forward guidance.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Analysts say the reason COST stock is falling after its latest print is that management failed to announce a one-time special dividend payment. Investors have come to expect special dividends from Costco and were expecting one again at the end of this year, only to be disappointed when none was announced.

The Seattle-based warehouse club announced a special dividend of $15 per share in December 2023. It also issued a one-time $10 per share dividend in December 2020, a $7 special dividend in 2017, and a $5 dividend in 2015. Analysts say that, based on the pattern, another special payout is due to COST stockholders.

Costco pays a regular quarterly dividend of $1.31 per share, giving the stock a yield of 0.60%.

Costco's Valuation and Cash Position

Costco's strong cash position has allowed the retailer to be generous with shareholders over the years, issuing special payouts about once every two years. However, the grocery retailer may have been too generous in the past, setting expectations too high in the process. Costco currently has about $15 billion of cash on hand.

Some analysts say the lack of a special dividend may not be the only reason COST stock continues to underperform the broader market. Valuation is also likely an issue for many investors. Costco's stock currently trades at 47 times future earnings estimates, which is rich, especially for a retailer.

Is COST Stock a Buy?

Costco Wholesale's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 24 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 16 Buy and eight Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average COST price target of $1,091.79 implies 25.16% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Why Michael Burry Sees Weakness in the U.S. Banking System
data.description:
Michael Burry, the investor known for predicting the 2008 housing crash, believes that the U.S. banking system is showing serious signs of weakness. After the Federal Reserve's latest meeting, Burry pointed out that the Fed now considers its reserve balances "ample" and will begin buying short-term U.S. Treasuries regularly. This process, starting December 12 at about $40 billion a month, is meant to keep liquidity flowing in the financial system, similar to how quantitative easing once worked.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

These ongoing Treasury purchases help banks meet reserve requirements by injecting more money into the system. While this isn't exactly the same as the large-scale stimulus used during past crises, it still boosts the money supply. However, Burry finds it concerning that the Fed needs to do this at all. He noted that if U.S. banks can't operate without more than $3 trillion in reserves or Fed support, it suggests that the system is fragile, not strong. For reference, current reserves stand at about $2.8 trillion.

Nevertheless, stocks had rallied after the Fed's announcement, partly due to hopes that these actions would stabilize banks. According to JPMorgan (JPM), the optimism surrounding the Fed's decision helped markets move higher. Still, Burry isn't convinced. Therefore, he's avoiding bank stocks and keeping his money in cash or Treasury money market funds. Separately, Burry has also warned about a potential AI stock bubble and made bets against major tech names like Nvidia (NVDA).

Is SPY Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) based on 420 Buys, 76 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average SPY price target of $794.84 per share implies 16.7% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: TLRY, ACB, CGC: Why Are Cannabis Stocks Up Today?
data.description:
Shares of cannabis stocks, Tilray Brands (TLRY), Aurora Cannabis (ACB), and Canopy Growth (CGC), are surging on Friday after reports indicated that the U.S. government is preparing to significantly ease federal restrictions on marijuana. As of this writing, CGC stock is up 35%, TLRY has gained 30%, and ACB is up 14.5%.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Trump Pushes Plan to Loosen Federal Marijuana Rules

On Thursday, a report by The Washington Post said President Donald Trump is expected to direct federal agencies to reclassify marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III. Schedule I substances like heroin and LSD are considered to have no accepted medical use and a high potential for abuse, while Schedule III drugs have recognized medical uses and a lower risk of abuse.

Trump reportedly spoke with House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-Louisiana) during a Wednesday call from the Oval Office to discuss the plan. According to sources, the president is preparing an executive order that would instruct federal agencies to move forward with reclassifying marijuana, making it easier to access.

However, a White House spokesperson told Forbes that no final decisions have been made regarding marijuana rescheduling.

What It Means for Cannabis Stocks

A potential move to reclassify marijuana to Schedule III would be a major turning point for U.S. cannabis companies. It would ease federal restrictions, reduce tax burdens, and make it easier for businesses to access banking, financing, and insurance. Additionally, the shift would reduce regulatory hurdles, open the door for broader medical and commercial use, and potentially draw more institutional investors into the sector.

For stocks like TLRY, CGC, and ACB, the news signals a path toward a more legitimate, less restricted operating environment —which is why shares reacted so sharply. If rescheduling goes forward, it could mark the beginning of a more stable, scalable future for the entire cannabis sector.

data.title: Why Did Rivian Stock (RIVN) Jump 18 Today%?
data.description:
Shares in American electric vehicle (EV) producer Rivian Automotive (RIVN) climbed as high as 18% on Friday morning despite a mixed Wall Street reaction to its first Autonomy and AI Day.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

During the event, the California-based company disclosed that it has built its own custom chip, a new vehicle computer, and AI models with the end goal of integrating self-driving features into its future vehicles. Rivian plans to launch Autonomy+, its new self-driving software subscription, in 2026. The software is powered by its in-house processors and computer systems.

Needham Cheers Clearer Sign of Progress

Reacting to the event, Needham analyst Chris Pierce raised his RIVN price target by over 64% from $14 to $23 per share, noting that the carmaker — which has turned to job cuts to reduce its cost base — is showing clearer signs of progress across its tech roadmap. 

Pierce also argued that the EV maker now has clear growth drivers in software, autonomy, and upcoming R2 sport-utility vehicle (SUV) launch. He believes that these could together help move its stock price higher over time.

This bullish view may be fueling the latest rally, as investors were similarly upbeat heading into the event, with high hopes for the company's turn to artificial intelligence.

RBC Capital Points to Liquidity Concerns

The rally on Friday means that investors are ignoring warnings from other analysts, such as Tom Narayan from RBC Capital.

Narayan argued that while Rivian's latest product offerings improve its lineup, they do not address concerns about the company's liquidity and the profitability of its upcoming R2 SUV and the planned compact electric hatchback R3.

Consequently, the RBC Capital analyst reaffirmed his Hold rating on RIVN, with his price target of $14 per share implying about 27% downside risk. Moreover, while Goldman Sachs' Mark Delaney raised his price target from $13 to $16, he also stuck to his Neutral rating.

Delaney's new price target implies about 16% downside risk. The analyst pointed out that Rivian's new strategy showed the carmaker is looking to boost its profit margin with advanced software and self-driving technology.

Is RIVN Stock a Good Buy?

Across Wall Street, Rivian's shares still have a Hold consensus recommendation based on five Buys, eight Holds, and six Sells issued by 19 analysts over the past three months.

Moreover, the average RIVN price target of $14.82 implies about 22% downside risk.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: 'Don't Fall for the Hype,' Warn Analysts about Rivian's Robotaxi Plans
data.description:
Rivian Automotive (RIVN) caught the attention of analysts after its AI & Autonomy Day event, where the EV company introduced its own self-driving chip, a third-generation computer system, and an in-car AI called Rivian Assistant. It also discussed a new "Large Driving Model" that powers hands-free driving across 3.5 million miles of roads in North America. This feature, available through a paid Autonomy+ subscription, will continue to expand as Rivian plans to add LiDAR and radar to future vehicles. The long-term goal is to reach Level 4 autonomy, which could support robotaxi services.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Although investors seem excited (shares are up 16% at the time of writing), Morgan Stanley (MS) remains cautious about Rivian's future. Five-star analyst Andrew Percoco said that Rivian is building the tools it needs to stay competitive in the next generation of cars. However, he warned that the company still faces serious risks, such as weak demand, which could limit the driving data needed to improve its self-driving systems and reach profitability. As a result, Morgan Stanley continues to rate the stock as Sell, with a price target of $12 per share.

Separately, Wells Fargo (WFC) had a more neutral view on Rivian with a Hold rating. Analyst Colin Langan said that the recent excitement around the stock shows that people are hopeful about Rivian's bold technology, product design, and strong partnerships. Still, he pointed out that the company has very little room to make mistakes. In addition, with limited experience making and selling vehicles, Rivian must show that it can attract customers without spending too much on marketing in order to achieve long-term success.

Is RIVN Stock a Buy or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on RIVN stock based on five Buys, eight Holds, and six Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average RIVN price target of $13.87 per share implies 28.9% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
